description: Full-stack dual-role grocery inventory and order management platform — sellers list products, buyers purchase them. React frontend, FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL, Docker Compose, deployed on Render + Vercel.

userStories.title: Database schema and migrations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all database tables created so the application can persist users, products, customers, orders, order items, and complaints.
acceptanceCriteria: Create users table: id, full_name, email, phone, password_hash (nullable), google_id (nullable), role (buyer/seller), created_at
Create products table: id, name, sku (unique), sui_number (unique random), price, quantity, seller_id (FK to users), created_at, updated_at
Create customers table: id, user_id (FK to users, unique), full_name, email (unique), phone, created_at
Create orders table: id, customer_id (FK to customers), total_amount, status, created_at
Create order_items table: id, order_id (FK to orders), product_id (FK to products), quantity, unit_price, subtotal
Create complaints table: id, order_item_id (FK to order_items), customer_id (FK to customers), product_id (FK to products), message, status (open/resolved), created_at
All migrations run successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Email/password authentication (backend)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need email/password signup and login endpoints so users can authenticate with traditional credentials.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /auth/signup accepts full_name, email, phone, password, role; validates unique email; hashes password with bcrypt; returns JWT
POST /auth/login accepts email, password; verifies credentials; returns JWT with user_id, role
Password strength validation (min 8 chars)
Proper error messages for duplicate email and invalid credentials
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Google OAuth authentication (backend)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Google OAuth endpoints so users can sign in with their Gmail account.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /auth/google accepts Google ID token; verifies with Google; if user exists by google_id, issue JWT; if new, create user with role selection pending
PATCH /auth/role accepts role (buyer/seller) for first-time OAuth users; updates user record
JWT contains same payload structure as email/password auth
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Login and landing page (frontend)
userStories.description: As a user, I want a landing page with two role cards and a login screen supporting both email/password and Google OAuth so I can sign in conveniently.
acceptanceCriteria: Landing page shows two large cards: 'I am a Buyer' and 'I am a Seller'
Clicking a card navigates to login screen pre-selected for that role
Login screen has email + password text fields
Login screen has Sign in with Google button
Login screen has link to signup form
Signup form collects: full name, email, phone, password, role (pre-selected)
Google OAuth flow redirects to Google, then back with role selection on first login
Form validation with inline error messages
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Product CRUD (backend)
userStories.description: As a seller, I want to create, read, update, and delete my products so I can manage my inventory.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /products — seller only; accepts name, sku, price, quantity; auto-generates unique random SUI number; auto-links seller_id from JWT
GET /products — returns all products for buyers; returns only seller's products for sellers
GET /products/{id} — returns product by ID
PUT /products/{id} — seller only, own products only; updates allowed fields
DELETE /products/{id} — seller only, own products only
SUI number is a unique random integer (8-12 digits)
SKU uniqueness enforced
Proper error handling and HTTP status codes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Product management UI (frontend)
userStories.description: As a seller, I want a product management page in my dashboard so I can add, edit, and delete my products.
acceptanceCriteria: Seller dashboard has Products section with table/grid of own products
Add Product button opens form/modal with fields: name, SKU, price, quantity
SUI number auto-generated and shown read-only after creation
Edit button opens pre-filled form for each product
Delete button shows confirmation dialog before deleting
Stock badge colors: green > 10, yellow <= 10, red <= 5
Success/error toasts for all actions
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Product grid and search (frontend)
userStories.description: As a buyer, I want to browse all products with a search bar and category filters so I can find items to purchase.
acceptanceCriteria: Product grid with cards showing name, price, stock indicator, add button
Search bar filters products by name
Category chips for horizontal scrollable filtering
Cart icon in header with badge showing item count
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Cart drawer and order placement UI (frontend)
userStories.description: As a buyer, I want a cart drawer that shows my selected items and a Place Order button so I can complete my purchase.
acceptanceCriteria: Cart drawer slides out showing items, quantities, line totals, grand total
Place Order button in cart drawer
Cart icon in header updates badge count as items are added/removed
Successful order shows confirmation and clears cart
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Order placement (backend)
userStories.description: As a buyer, I want to place an order so my selected items are purchased and stock is updated.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /orders — buyer only; accepts list of {product_id, quantity}; validates stock availability
Auto-calculates total amount from product prices and quantities
Automatically reduces stock for each product atomically
Auto-onboards buyer as customer if not already a customer
Returns order details with items and total
Error if stock insufficient for any item — no partial order created
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Order history UI (frontend)
userStories.description: As a buyer, I want to view my past orders with status badges so I can track my purchases.
acceptanceCriteria: Order history page lists all past orders
Each order shows: date, items, total, status badge
Click order to expand/show detail
File Complaint button on past order items
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Seller analytics API (backend)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a seller analytics endpoint so the dashboard can display performance metrics.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /seller/analytics returns: total_products_listed, total_units_sold, total_revenue, low_stock_products (qty <= 5)
Results scoped to authenticated seller
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Seller analytics dashboard (frontend)
userStories.description: As a seller, I want an analytics dashboard with stat cards so I can see my performance at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Four stat cards: Products Listed, Units Sold, Revenue, Low Stock
Low stock products listed with name and quantity
Data fetched from /seller/analytics endpoint
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Complaints API (backend)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need complaints endpoints so buyers can file issues and sellers can resolve them.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /complaints — buyer only; accepts order_item_id, message; linked to product and customer
GET /seller/complaints — returns complaints on seller's products with product name, customer name, message, status
PATCH /complaints/{id}/resolve — seller only; marks complaint as resolved
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Complaints UI (frontend)
userStories.description: As a buyer, I want to file complaints on orders; as a seller, I want to view and resolve complaints.
acceptanceCriteria: Buyers see File Complaint button on past order items — clicking opens a form to submit message
Sellers see complaints panel showing: product name, customer name, message, status badge
Resolve button on open complaints — clicking marks as resolved and updates UI
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Docker containerization
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the entire system containerized so it runs consistently across environments.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend Dockerfile using slim Python image
Frontend Dockerfile with multi-stage build and nginx for production
.dockerignore files for backend and frontend
docker-compose.yml with frontend, backend, postgres services
Named volume for PostgreSQL data persistence
Environment variables via .env file (no hardcoded credentials)
docker-compose up starts all services successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Deployment to Render and Vercel
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the backend on Render and frontend on Vercel so the system is publicly accessible.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend Docker image deployed to Render with env vars configured
Frontend deployed to Vercel pointing to Render backend URL
CORS configured for frontend domain
Both URLs publicly accessible
End-to-end flow works on deployed URLs
